Title: 13th Year of Blogging
Description: I've been blogging for 13 years. So much has changed, yet everything is the same. The only thing AI cannot replicate is longevity. Plant your seeds right now.

Title: Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor
Description: Thoughts on an experiment that I've been running for about a month.

Title: Software Engineering Is Becoming Civil Engineering
Description: I gave a guest lecture on AI in Michael Hilton’s Foundations of Software Engineering course (CMU 17-313) today. One of my favorite things about lecturing is the conversations that happen afterward, the ones that go in directions nobody planned.

Title: Optimizing for understanding
Description: When code review is the biggest bottleneck, you need to optimize for understanding.

Title: Signals, the push-pull based algorithm — Willy Brauner
Description: We have been using Signals in production for years via several modern front-end frameworks like Solid, Vue, and others, but few of us are able to explain how they work internally. I wanted to dig into it, especially diving deep into the push-pull based algorithm, the core mechanism behind their reactivity.

Title: Kasava - The Agentic Platform for Product Engineers
Description: Kasava is the AI-native platform purpose-built for product development. Plan, build, and ship with AI-powered chat, call analysis, and plan generation.

Title: Why You (Probably) Don't Need Static Typing in Rails
Description: Static typing in Ruby sounds appealing — but good naming conventions get you 80% of the benefits with none of the overhead. No gems, no annotations, no build step.

Title: Paste clipboard images into Claude Code over SSH
Description: Claude Code can read images, but not when you're connected over SSH. claude-ssh-image-skill bridges that gap with a local daemon, a Go client, and an SSH reverse tunnel.

Title: Reading leaked Claude Code source code
Description: Interesting findings from reading 132,000 lines of TypeScript that power Claude Code. The post uses the repo at https://github.com/chatgptprojects/claude-code to reference code, note that it might go down by the time you are reading this.

Title: The Struggle is What We Crave
Description: On the death of web dev communities, the callouses that made us better, and why eliminating struggle might be the worst thing we've ever built.

Title: Speed Is Only Useful If You're Going in the Right Direction — Matthew Boston
Description: Velocity is a vector — it has magnitude and direction. AI tools make it trivially easy to build the wrong thing fast. The bottleneck was never typing speed; it was always understanding.

Title: Why Are There So Many Dead Posts on Hacker News | Kruno Golubić
Description: Some platforms are silently banned on Hacker News. Your posts go dead and you never know. If this matters to you, here's what you can do about it.

Title: Birthday Slideshow | Joe IQ
Description: Throwing a birthday party for yourself is the perfect way to get your favorite people in one room. And while guests have a great time during the party, the joy doesn’t stop when it’s over: they’re still raving about your slideshow days later. What slideshow? I can explain…

Title: Agent Frameworks Are Getting Squeezed
Description: AI labs are building down into orchestration. Automation platforms are building up into reasoning. Agent frameworks are caught in a compression event from both sides.

Title: I Am a Cross-Cutting Concern — Scott Lawson
Description: Everything I've built in the last decade lives in one repo. Not because the projects are related, but because I am.

Title: Very small engines

Title: DRAM pricing is killing the hobbyist SBC market
Description:
Today Raspberry Pi announced more price increases for all Pis with LPDDR4 RAM, alongside a 'right-sized' 3GB RAM Pi 4 for $83.75.
The price increases bring the 16GB Pi 5 up to $299.99.
Despite today's date, this is not a joke.
I published a video going over the state of the hobbyist 'high end SBC' market (4/8/16 GB models in the current generation), which I'll embed below:

Title: Introducing EmDash — the spiritual successor to WordPress that solves plugin security
Description: Today we are launching the beta of EmDash, a full-stack serverless JavaScript CMS built on Astro 6.0. It combines the features of a traditional CMS with modern security, running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates.

Title: PrismML — Concentrating intelligence
Description: Large models can't fit on smartphones. Datacenters can't sustain them. PrismML is building ultra dense intelligence to solve both.
